channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Appoints New Ambassadors to Strengthen Diplomatic Relations
item.description: Dar es Salaam — President Samia Suluhu Hassan has made several changes in Tanzania’s diplomatic corps to improve the country’s representation abroad. According to the Chief Secretary, Dr Moses Kusiluk...

item.title: Middle East conflict intensifies as Israel and Iran exchange strikes
item.description: Israel has launched another wave of broad-scale air strikes on Tehran as the conflict in the Middle East enters its second week, intensifying regional tensions and causing global economic concern. The...

item.title: Pag-IBIG urged to release P630 million escrow funds to Globe Asiatique for completed housing units
item.description: The Home Development Mutual Fund (Pag-IBIG) has been requested to release P630 million in escrow funds to Globe Asiatique Realty Holdings Corp. following the dismissal of an estafa case against its of...

item.title: Pakistan security forces conduct successful intelligence-based operations in Balochistan, killing 15 terrorists
item.description: On 5th March 2026, Pakistan’s security forces carried out two separate intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in the Harnai and Basima districts of Balochistan, resulting in the elimination of 15 terror...

item.title: Daryl Hannah denounces inaccurate portrayal in Ryan Murphy’s Love Story series
item.description: Daryl Hannah has publicly criticised Ryan Murphy’s latest series, Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette, for misrepresenting her character. The series, which chronicles the high-profile r...

item.title: Iran Faces Widespread Internet Shutdown Amid Conflict and Infrastructure Damage
item.description: Since late February 2026, Iran has experienced a nearly complete internet blackout affecting approximately 90 million people, following U.S. and Israeli airstrikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khame...

item.title: Quebec teacher contests provincial policy on student gender identity and parental notification
item.description: A high school teacher in Quebec, identified only as A.B., has taken the provincial government to court over a policy that permits students aged 14 and older to change their name and pronouns at school...

item.title: Russia and Belarus Compete at Paralympics Amid Boycotts by Several Nations
item.description: Russia and Belarus have made their return to the Paralympic Games, with their national flags displayed for the first time in over a decade. The International Paralympic Committee (IPC) allowed 10 athl...

item.title: PTI condemns fuel price hike, urges government to ease public burden
item.description: On Saturday, Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) strongly condemned the government’s decision to increase petrol and diesel prices by Rs55 per litre, calling it an 'inflation bomb' for ordinary citizens. T...

item.title: Opposition urges govt to reverse fuel price hike amid rising inflation
item.description: On Saturday, the opposition strongly criticised the government’s recent decision to increase petrol and diesel prices by Rs55 per litre, labelling it an 'inflation bomb' for ordinary citizens. The fue...

item.title: Southern African liberation movements focus on economic growth and social development
item.description: During the 12th Meeting of Secretaries General of Southern Africa’s liberation movements held in Kibaha, Tanzania, leaders from six major liberation parties pledged to strengthen regional cooperation ...

item.title: St. Boniface Hospital emergency department faces record wait times amid systemic challenges
item.description: Emergency physicians at St. Boniface Hospital in Winnipeg have raised serious concerns about the ongoing long wait times in the emergency department, which they say have become normalized across Manit...

item.title: Harry Styles donates 100 tickets to pupils at his former school in Cheshire
item.description: Harry Styles has gifted 100 tickets to pupils from his former school, Holmes Chapel Comprehensive in Cheshire, to attend his one-off concert at the Co-op Live Arena in Manchester. The tickets were all...

item.title: India Prepares Affordable Generic Ozempic for Diabetes and Weight Management
item.description: With the patent on semaglutide, commercially known as Ozempic, expiring on 20th March, Indian pharmaceutical companies like Sun Pharma, Zydus Lifesciences, Dr Reddy's, and Natco Pharma are gearing up ...

item.title: Major World Events Captured in Photos from February 28 to March 6, 2026
item.description: From February 28 to March 6, 2026, the world witnessed significant events captured in a series of striking images. The Middle East was rocked by a new conflict after the United States and Israel launc...

item.title: Ghana prepares alternative gold refining routes amid Dubai flight disruptions
item.description: Ghana, one of Africa's biggest gold producers, is making plans to move its artisanal gold refining away from Dubai if flight disruptions to the UAE continue. Dubai normally handles about 80% of Ghana'...

item.title: EJ Obiena Wins ISTAF Indoor Berlin with 5.78m Season-Best Vault
item.description: Filipino pole vaulter EJ Obiena regained his winning form at the ISTAF Indoor meet in Berlin, Germany, on March 6, 2026 (March 7 Manila time), clearing a season-best 5.78 meters. This marked his first...

item.title: Sixteen People Abducted in Kubwa, Including Vigilante’s Wife and Pastor’s Children
item.description: For Kubwa community inside Bwari Area Council of Abuja, trouble don come as heavily armed gunmen invade Peze community on Tuesday night between 9:30 p.m. and 10 p.m., moving from house to house dey tr...

item.title: Nairobi Floods Disrupt Roads and Trigger Debate on Urban Drainage Management
item.description: Nairobi experienced heavy rains on the evening of March 6, 2026, which caused widespread flooding across key areas including South B, South C, Nairobi West, Kilimani, and the CBD. Roads like Uhuru Hig...

item.title: Durban firefighters contain major fire at Springfield China Mall, 12 shops lost
item.description: Early on Saturday morning, a severe fire broke out at the Springfield China Mall in Durban, destroying 12 trading units and severely damaging two others. The eThekwini Fire Services Directorate respon...
